geographic_scope: subregional occupation cluster west and northwest of Kyiv city
theater_description: Russian advance and occupation belt on the Hostomel-Bucha-Irpin axis and adjacent settlements northwest of Kyiv during the failed encirclement of the capital.
geometry_method_note: Footprint should be the contiguous built-up and peri-urban occupation belt from Hostomel through Bucha and Irpin, not all Kyiv Oblast. A later GIS pass should clip to occupied settlements and dates rather than use a static oblast polygon.
military_area_note: Would need Russian assault elements and Ukrainian defenders specific to the cluster, not all forces on the northern front around Kyiv.
civilian_area_note: Would count only civilians physically present in occupied/contested settlements; rapid outflow from Bucha/Irpin makes a static pre-war stock misleading.
split_reason: Occupation killings in the suburban Kyiv belt are not denominator-compatible with all-Kyiv or all-northern-Ukraine totals.
quality_note: Strong civilian-harm attribution to Russian forces, but no clean cluster-wide civilian direct-death total and no theater-matched military exposure denominator.

theater_description: Siege and bombardment of Chernihiv city and its immediately contiguous defended approaches during the northeastern arm of the failed Russian drive on Kyiv.
geometry_method_note: Use the urban municipality and directly connected defended approaches; do not use all of Chernihiv Oblast. Civilian exposure is the population physically present in the city during the siege window.
military_area_note: Military denominator refers to Ukrainian defenders directly assigned to the city defense, anchored on the 1st Tank Brigade and reinforced by territorial defence and reservists.
civilian_area_note: Civilian denominator starts from the city's pre-war resident population and is reduced over time using end-March remaining-population evidence.
split_reason: Chernihiv's siege was geographically and operationally distinct from the broader northeastern front and has city-bounded civilian-harm and population evidence.
quality_note: Civilian side is reasonably well bounded; military deaths are residual and defender person-months are partly modeled rather than source-direct.

theater_description: Encirclement, bombardment, street fighting, and final reduction of Mariupol city, including the port, city districts, and the Azovstal/Illich redoubts.
geometry_method_note: Use Mariupol city only, not all of Donetsk Oblast or the broader southern front. Geometry changed sharply as the city became encircled and district-level access collapsed.
military_area_note: Would need Ukrainian garrison presence over time (36th Marine Brigade, Azov Regiment and attached defenders) rather than broader southeastern front totals.
civilian_area_note: Civilian denominator must reflect severe entrapment and outflow: very large numbers remained early in March, but the city's physically present population collapsed across the siege.
split_reason: Mariupol's siege mortality and displacement dynamics are unique and cannot be represented with oblast- or national-level denominators.
quality_note: Direct civilian floor is source-grounded, but military deaths and the direct-versus-mixed mortality boundary remain too fragile for a full DR.

theater_description: Initial frontal attacks, urban defense, and prolonged bombardment of Kharkiv city until Russian forces were pushed back from the city in May 2022.
geometry_method_note: Use Kharkiv city proper, not all of Kharkiv Oblast. A later GIS pass could split inner-city bombardment from northern suburban contact zones.
military_area_note: Would need a city-phase force-denominator series rather than all eastern-front or entire Kharkiv-oblast force totals.
civilian_area_note: Civilian exposure should be the city population physically present during the bombardment window; the denominator fell as large numbers evacuated.
split_reason: Kharkiv city was a distinct urban-defense episode with much better civilian-harm evidence than the wider eastern front.
quality_note: Civilian side is reasonably bounded, but available military-loss claims are partial, casualty-heavy, or not clearly death-only.

geographic_scope: subregional campaign on the west bank of the Dnipro in Kherson Oblast
theater_description: Ukrainian interdiction and ground pressure campaign against Russian forces on the right/west bank of the Dnipro culminating in the Russian withdrawal from Kherson city.
geometry_method_note: Footprint should be the Russian-held west-bank zone in Kherson Oblast, not the whole oblast or all southern Ukraine. Control lines shifted as bridges were cut and settlements changed hands.
military_area_note: Would need west-bank Russian and Ukrainian force exposures rather than all southern-theater forces.
civilian_area_note: Civilian denominator would require a time-varying west-bank resident stock after heavy displacement from Kherson city and nearby settlements.
split_reason: The west-bank campaign differs materially from the earlier occupation phase and from post-liberation shelling east of the river.
quality_note: Displacement evidence exists, but the repair pass did not recover a matched west-bank direct-civilian-death series or campaign-specific force person-months.

theater_description: Long attritional fight for Bakhmut city and the immediate operational pocket around it, including rotation routes and surrounding villages tied directly to the defense of the city.
geometry_method_note: Any later full estimate should use the city and immediate pocket actually tied to holding Bakhmut, not the whole Donbas front.
military_area_note: Would require side-specific troop rotations and exposure over a very long attritional window with multiple force packages.
civilian_area_note: Civilian exposure collapsed dramatically as the population fled, meaning a simple static population denominator would be highly misleading.
split_reason: Bakhmut's duration, rotation pattern, and front-contamination make it unsuitable for a whole-front denominator.
quality_note: Published numbers often mix killed and wounded or spill over beyond the pocket; denominator logic remains too weak for promotion.
